mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 4.5 percent signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending expectations, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in more than 55 percent of triggered monthly observations over a 5-year sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand expansion, which drives corporate earnings revisions upward and produces positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in both series fails to produce positive 60-day forward returns in more than 60 percent of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding two percent annualized during periods of elevated inflation signals capacity constraints that force the central bank to tighten policy aggressively, causing negative monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth above two percent yields positive 20-day forward returns in more than 50 percent of high-inflation observations, or regime-conditional alpha is negative.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index readings surpassing three percent trigger inflation hedging flows that rotate capital out of equities into real assets, producing negative weekly S&P 500 returns regardless of growth conditions.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55 percent of triggered months, or the threshold model shows no predictive power (AIC/BIC favors null model).

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls directly increases aggregate wage income and household purchasing power, which supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive quarterly equity market returns.
failure_condition: PAYEMS month-over-month growth shows zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward returns over a 5-year out-of-sample testing window.

mechanism_narrative: A positive divergence where monthly retail sales growth outpaces quarterly GDP growth by more than one point signals accelerating consumer momentum that translates into positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: The retail-GDP growth spread fails to predict 20-day return direction with accuracy above 52 percent in out-of-sample testing, or interaction coefficient is insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below its 2017 baseline confirms a supply-side contraction that forces earnings downgrades and produces negative quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO below 100 yields positive 60-day forward returns in more than 55 percent of cases, or the signal loses predictive power when controlling for monetary policy shocks.
